title: Claude Fable 5 is here!
summary: YouTube video reviewing Claude Fable 5 (also referred to as Claude Mythos 5) with demonstrations across multiple domains including ray tracing, 3D reconstruction, music composition, and chemistry. The review compares Fable 5 performance against GPT 5.5 and discusses model benchmarks and specifications.
writeup: This video stress-tests Claude 4.5 across a suite of demanding agentic coding benchmarks, from a from-scratch ray-tracing renderer to a fully interactive 3D digital twin of Earth, to establish where the model leads and where it falls short relative to GPT-4.5. The through-line is that Claude 4.5 is purpose-built for agentic workflows: it self-verifies its output after each round, catches and repairs its own bugs, and consistently delivers in one to two prompts what previously required iteration-heavy sessions. Honest failure cases are included alongside the wins, making the comparison substantive rather than promotional.
key_points: Claude 4.5 is the first model to correctly locate and circle a hidden frog in a photograph by splitting the image into quadrants, inspecting each region, and pinpointing exact coordinates, a visual reasoning task no prior model had solved.

In Claude Code, Claude 4.5 built a from-scratch ray-tracing simulation (sphere, cube, pyramid with fully interactive reflectivity, roughness, transparency, and IOR controls) in two prompts and without any external libraries, validating its underlying physics and lighting understanding.

A one-prompt 3D digital twin of Earth succeeded fully: seamless zoom from orbit to street level, per-country hover stats (population, area, GDP), cloud cover, live flight traffic trails, a day-night terminator, and night-mode city lights.

Claude 4.5 auto-verifies its own fixes after each code iteration and runs browser tests to catch residual errors; this agentic self-checking makes outputs more robust but consumes significantly more tokens than non-verifying models.

The 3D office-scene reconstruction from a reference photograph was judged a failure: desk and desk-occupant positions diverged from the source image despite two rounds of correction, though the result was more coherent than the GPT-4.5 equivalent, which produced many disconnected pieces.

Claude 4.5 generated a fully functional DAW interface with five instrument tracks, piano-roll note editing, and standard mixer controls in a single prompt, and then composed a 32-bar expressive song on request.
